resources.string: One Quest app for settings shortcuts, wireless ADB pairing, and the upstream MBF web UI.
FAQ, fix form, and MBF access in one place, styled after the live Sm0ke wiki tools.
Reconnect if needed. Android dev mode: %1$s. Wireless Debugging: %2$s.
Launch MBF, jump to the support form, or reopen setup from the wider home screen.
Loading live FAQ from the wiki…
Could not load the live FAQ right now. You can still open the full FAQ page.
The live FAQ and fix form stay available during setup.
Check both requirements before continuing. This setup path requires Multiple Accounts to be off and the headset unlock code or PIN to be off.
I confirmed there are not Multiple Accounts on this headset.
I confirmed the headset unlock code or PIN is off.
This app will try to open Settings App Info automatically. When the popup window appears, click Open. Then continue to the next step and open About headset manually.
Open About and tap Build Number 7 times
Scroll down and click About headset, scroll down, and click Build Number 7 times to enable Android developer mode on the headset. You cannot continue until Android developer mode is enabled.
This app will try to open Developer Settings automatically. If it does not, use the button below. Once that page opens, continue to the next step so Wireless Debugging can open.
Click on Wireless Debugging (the text itself) from Developer Settings. You cannot continue until Wireless Debugging is enabled and its submenu is open.
Choose Pair with pairing code on the headset, then enter the 6-digit code and pairing port here manually and press Pair Device.
Connect with the wireless debug port
After pairing, the headset shows a wireless debug port. Press Detect Debug Port to try to fill it, or enter it manually, then press Connect Device. The status line should change once ADB is ready.
Once connected, press Launch MBF In This App. That starts the upstream MBF bridge locally and loads the live MBF web UI in the built-in browser.
Replace this with a screenshot of the first guide screen showing the Multiple Accounts and unlock-code checks.
Replace this with a screenshot of the Settings app popup where the user should click Open.
Replace this with a screenshot of the About page where Build Number is visible.
Replace this with a screenshot of Quest Developer Settings where Wireless Debugging is visible.
Replace this with a screenshot of the Wireless Debugging page after it is enabled.
Replace this with a screenshot of the Pair with pairing code dialog showing the code and pairing port.
Replace this with a screenshot of the Wireless Debugging page showing the active wireless debug port.
Replace this with a screenshot of the final guide step showing the built-in MBF launch controls.
Pair and connect your headset first, then launch MBF inside this app.
ADB is connected. You can launch MBF directly in this app.
ADB control permissions are already available, so this app can keep driving the MBF setup flow itself.
This app starts the upstream MBF bridge locally, then opens the live MBF web app in an embedded browser. The web UI continues updating from the upstream MBF site.

Open Wireless Debugging on the headset, choose Pair with pairing code, enter the pairing code and pairing port manually, then press Detect Debug Port or enter the wireless debug port yourself before connecting.
This clears the saved pairing port, wireless debug port, and game package for this app. It does not change headset system settings.
Recent setup actions and detection results appear here.
Enter a 6-digit pairing code and a valid pairing port.
Pairing succeeded. Detect or enter the wireless debug port next.
Could not detect the wireless debug port automatically.
Enter a valid wireless debug port.
Connect the headset before launching MBF.
Failed to start the integrated MBF bridge.
